howTo.name: How to convert PNG to JPG
howTo.description: Step-by-step guide to converting PNG files to JPG format using this free tool.

steps.name: Add PNG files
steps.text: Drag PNG files onto the drop zone or click to browse your device. You can add multiple files at once.

steps.name: Set JPG quality
steps.text: Use the quality slider (60–95%) to choose the balance between file size and image quality. The default 85% works well for most photos.

steps.name: Download JPG files
steps.text: After conversion, download files individually or all at once. Each file shows its original and converted size.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert PNG to JPG?
contentBlocks.html:
PNG is a lossless format that produces large files — a 3000×2000 pixel screenshot can weigh 5–15 MB. JPG uses lossy compression to create files that are 5–10× smaller with minimal visible quality loss. For photographs and images without transparency, JPG is the more practical format.

JPG is universally supported by every browser, email client, social media platform, and image editor. At quality 85–90, JPG produces visually identical results to PNG for photographic content while being dramatically smaller. Converting PNG screenshots and photos to JPG saves storage and makes files easier to share.

Note that JPG does not support transparency — transparent areas in your PNG will be filled with a solid background color (typically white). If you need transparency, keep your files as PNG or convert to WebP instead.

This converter processes files locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server. No registration, no limits, no watermarks.

contentBlocks.title: How to convert PNG to JPG
contentBlocks.description: The conversion takes just a few seconds:

items.title: 1. Add PNG files
items.description: Drag your PNG images onto the upload area or click to select them from your device. There is no limit on the number of files.

items.title: 2. Set quality
items.description: Choose the JPG quality level (default 85%). A lower value produces a smaller file. For photographs, 80–85% is the sweet spot.

items.title: 3. Download JPG
items.description: Once conversion is complete, download files one by one or all at once. Each file displays how much space you saved.

contentBlocks.title: When should you convert PNG to JPG?
contentBlocks.description: PNG-to-JPG conversion is useful in many real-world scenarios:

items.title: Email attachments
items.description: PNG photos can be 5–15 MB — too large for most email providers. JPG reduces file size by 5–10×, making photos easy to attach and send without exceeding mailbox limits.

items.title: Marketplace listings
items.description: Platforms like eBay, Amazon, and Etsy often prefer or require JPG product images. Converting PNG screenshots or renders to JPG ensures compatibility.

items.title: Online forms and applications
items.description: Government portals, job applications, and banking forms frequently accept only JPG with a size limit. A quick conversion solves the problem.

items.title: Websites and blogs
items.description: JPG photos load faster than PNGs, improving page speed and Core Web Vitals scores. For even smaller files, consider converting to WebP.

contentBlocks.title: What makes this PNG to JPG converter different?

items.title: Complete privacy

items.title: No file limit

items.title: Quality control

contentBlocks.title: Tips for choosing the right JPG quality
contentBlocks.html:
The ideal JPG quality setting depends on what you plan to do with the image:

  • 90–95% – Archival or print. Minimal compression, maximum visual fidelity.
  • 80–85% – General use (web, email, social media). The best balance of size and quality.
  • 60–75% – Thumbnails or previews where file size matters more than detail.

This tool defaults to 85%, which covers the vast majority of use cases. You can adjust the slider at any time - even reconvert the same files with a different quality level.

contentBlocks.title: How much can you save by converting PNG to JPG?
contentBlocks.html:
PNG is lossless and therefore large for photographic content. JPG provides substantial savings:

Photo (PNG)

8.5 MB → 890 KB

Saving: ~90%

Screenshot

1.2 MB → 320 KB

Saving: ~73%

Social media graphic

2.4 MB → 450 KB

Saving: ~81%

Savings are highest for photographs and images with smooth gradients. Graphics with large flat color areas compress less efficiently in JPG.

contentBlocks.title: How image conversion impacts page speed and SEO
contentBlocks.html:
Core Web Vitals is a set of performance metrics Google uses when evaluating websites. One of them — LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) — measures the time until the largest visible element renders. On image-heavy pages, this is often a hero image or product photo.

Converting PNG photographs to JPG typically reduces file size by 5–10×, directly shortening download time and improving the LCP score. Combined with loading="lazy" and fetchpriority="high", this can significantly improve page performance.

For even smaller files, consider WebP (25–35% smaller than JPG) or AVIF (30–50% smaller). Use PageSpeed Insights to identify specific images worth optimizing.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about PNG to JPG conversion

items.question: Does converting PNG to JPG reduce image quality?
items.answer: JPG is a lossy format, so some quality loss is inevitable. At 80–85% quality the difference is virtually invisible to the naked eye. The tool lets you set quality from 60% to 95% so you can find the right balance.

items.question: What happens to PNG transparency?
items.answer: JPG does not support transparency. Transparent areas are replaced with a white background during conversion. If you need to preserve transparency, keep the PNG or convert to WebP instead.

items.question: What quality level should I choose?
items.answer: For photographs and screenshots, 80–85% gives a good compromise between size and appearance. For graphics with sharp edges (diagrams, text overlays), try 90–95% to avoid visible compression artefacts.

items.question: How much space can I save?
items.answer: Savings depend on image content. A 5–10 MB PNG photo converted to JPG at 85% quality typically drops to 0.3–1 MB - a reduction of 80–95%. Screenshots with large flat-colour areas save somewhat less.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes. Add as many files as you like - there is no limit. All images are converted in sequence, and you can download them individually or all at once.

items.question: Are my files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. The entire conversion runs locally in your browser via the Canvas API. Files never leave your device - they are not sent to any server. Data is cleared from memory when you close the page.

items.question: Does this tool work on mobile devices?
items.answer: Yes, but for the best experience we recommend a laptop or desktop. The converter uses the HTML5 Canvas API, which is supported by all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

items.question: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported. There is no limit on the total number of files you can convert in a single session.
